rows.answer: The binding limit is not the 36 GB of RAM. It is (a) the reasoning ceiling of a model small enough to run locally, and (b) error compounding across multi-step agent runs. Design around these [1].

The hard, non-negotiable constraints of Atelier are as follows:

| Constraint | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Hardware** | MacBook M3 Pro, 36 GB unified memory, macOS. This is the *only* machine. |
| **Cost** | $0. No purchases, no subscriptions,

The agent has two capability modes:

1. **Knowledge mode (research over my documents):** This mode ingests papers, notes, PDFs, and documents, retrieving and synthesizing answers grounded in them. It is verified using a graded Q&A evaluatio

1. Beating frontier cloud coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) [1].
2. "Magic" / fully autonomous general agent [1].
Atelier uses the `BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5` embedding model, and it runs on an M3 Pro device [3].
According to the table in your notes, a 13B-14B parameter model needs approximately 10 GB - 12 GB of memory [1].
